name: Waltham Chase
overview: Waltham Chase is a semi-rural village in the Winchester district of Hampshire, in the upper Hamble valley about 8-10 miles south-east of Winchester and neighbouring the town of Bishop's Waltham. It sits within the civil parish of Shedfield, with around 2,380 residents in the village. Once a forest where the Bishops of Waltham hunted game, it developed its own identity in the 19th century and now has a primary school, two pubs, a village store and the Waltham Chase Meadows SSSI on its eastern edge.
character: A friendly, community-minded semi-rural village with everyday amenities and protected meadows nearby, within easy reach of Bishop's Waltham.
amenities: Two public houses including The Black Dog
St John the Baptist CofE Primary School
Village hall used by many clubs and societies
Waltham Chase Meadows SSSI (unimproved neutral pasture)

schools.name: St John the Baptist CofE Primary School

prices.summary: A mix of period and modern family housing in a semi-rural setting; popular with families for the primary school and Swanmore College catchment. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks at Waltham Chase Meadows SSSI
Recreation ground and village green spaces
Easy access to Bishop's Waltham's shops and palace ruins
Countryside walks in the Hamble valley
pros: Primary school and Swanmore College catchment for families
Village store, pubs and village hall retained
Protected SSSI meadows on the doorstep
Close to Bishop's Waltham town amenities
cons: Lies along the busy Winchester Road (B2177)
Larger shopping requires Bishop's Waltham or beyond
whoItSuits: Families wanting an affordable, well-served semi-rural Hampshire village with good schooling near Bishop's Waltham.
primarySupermarket: Village store; supermarkets in Bishop's Waltham
